dagster-io__dagster.title: Asset dependencies across code locations
dagster-io__dagster.reason: Candidate fabricates nonexistent APIs (external_asset_version_query, ExternalAssetVersion) and misframes SourceAsset as legacy, missing the actual documented cross-location dependency approach.

dagster-io__dagster.title: How to handle assets with over 25,000 partitions
dagster-io__dagster.reason: The reference answer is that the limit was simply raised (100k OSS, 250k+), but the candidate never mentions this and instead proposes workarounds that miss the actual point.

dagster-io__dagster.title: Structured / json logging by default in Dagster+
dagster-io__dagster.reason: Candidate correctly notes no custom agent container is needed and logging is configured in the user code container, but it gets the key dagster.yaml structure wrong (python_logs/dagster_handler_config), uses incorrect formatter classes, and misses the Dagster JsonFormatter approach.

dagster-io__dagster.title: Change Dagster log level on runtime
dagster-io__dagster.reason: Candidate misses the documented run_config loggers approach and instead manipulates Python's logging which doesn't reliably control Dagster's built-in console logger level.

dagster-io__dagster.title: How do I set the repository arguments when using the LaunchRunMutation?
dagster-io__dagster.reason: Candidate correctly identifies repositoryLocationName as the code location name but fails to give the critical answer that repositoryName must be the literal string '__repository__', instead guessing '__main__'.

dagster-io__dagster.title: Examples for k8s configuration
dagster-io__dagster.reason: Candidate points to relevant K8s deployment/executor docs and concepts but misses the specific 'Customizing your Deployment' page and includes some inaccurate config/code details.

dagster-io__dagster.title: How can we mark partitions as "materialized" in dagster if the data already exists in the DWH?
dagster-io__dagster.reason: Candidate captures the general concept of reporting materializations without running code but misses the canonical UI 'Report materialization events' feature and the proper `report_runless_asset_event` API, instead suggesting fragile internal-API hacks.

dagster-io__dagster.title: What can I access from `context`?
dagster-io__dagster.reason: The candidate misidentifies the question as about the Claude Agent SDK rather than Dagster, fails to point to SolidExecutionContext/AbstractComputeExecutionContext or the docs link, and provides no correct information.

dagster-io__dagster.title: How can I install drivers like ODBC when deploying my code to Dagster+ Serverless?
dagster-io__dagster.reason: Candidate correctly suggests a custom image approach but misses the documented pre-install script/PEX-disable method, uses wrong dagster_cloud.yaml config, and invents a nonexistent 'dagster:latest-odbc' image.

dagster-io__dagster.title: Problem with dependencies of dbt_assets
dagster-io__dagster.reason: The candidate invents a non-existent `deps` parameter for `@dbt_assets` and misses the correct approach of declaring a dbt source with dagster asset_key meta plus a depends_on in the model.

dagster-io__dagster.title: How to access the context variable in a graph_multi_asset
dagster-io__dagster.reason: The candidate correctly identifies that context must be accessed within ops (not the graph) and gives accurate working examples matching the reference's core guidance.

dagster-io__dagster.title: How to migrate from using SourceAsset to AssetSpec
dagster-io__dagster.reason: Candidate uses incorrect API (AssetSpec io_manager_key param and @asset(spec=...)) instead of the correct metadata={'dagster/io_manager_key': ...} approach.

dagster-io__dagster.title: Dagster Essentials Data Link returns Not Found
dagster-io__dagster.reason: Reference identifies a known bug with a specific GitHub issue and CSV workaround; candidate offers only generic troubleshooting without the actual fix.

dagster-io__dagster.title: How to get the code location of a given asset?
dagster-io__dagster.reason: The candidate misses the actual API (context.get_step_execution_context().dagster_run...location_name) and instead suggests irrelevant inspect/module approaches that don't yield the code location name.

PrefectHQ__prefect.title: Unit testing @task decorated functions without active Flow context.
PrefectHQ__prefect.reason: The traceback is Prefect 1.x where the correct fix is task.run(); candidate never mentions .run() and instead recommends .fn (a Prefect 2.x feature), so it misses the reference answer.

PrefectHQ__prefect.title: Narrowing Variable Types
PrefectHQ__prefect.reason: The candidate correctly suggests cast and isinstance narrowing, but wrongly assumes Apache Airflow instead of Prefect, mixing in incorrect Airflow-specific imports and API signatures.

PrefectHQ__prefect.title: IncompleteRead Issue when trying to do a demo deploy
PrefectHQ__prefect.reason: Reference is a workaround for an undebuggable bug (build image separately, push=False), while candidate just asks for more info and suggests unrelated network changes.

PrefectHQ__prefect.title: Should we always wrap code with `@task`/`@flow` 🙏
PrefectHQ__prefect.reason: Candidate correctly conveys the key guidance—don't wrap everything, use @task for expensive/side-effect IO, keep pure code at root—and adds sound advice on avoiding globals, though it's more opinion/best-practice than strict docs.

PrefectHQ__prefect.title: Notification error with mattermost
PrefectHQ__prefect.reason: Candidate correctly identifies that an Apprise-specific scheme is needed but gives the wrong scheme/format (mattermost:// keeping hooks) versus the reference's mmosts:// with hooks removed and port specified.

PrefectHQ__prefect.title: Allow setting of task cache_policy globally
PrefectHQ__prefect.reason: The reference resolves it as a bug fixed in a later release; the candidate invents non-existent APIs/config like PREFECT_CACHE_POLICY and CachePolicy() and misses the actual fix.

PrefectHQ__prefect.title: Changed behaviour in the way `persist_result` works
PrefectHQ__prefect.reason: Candidate fails to confirm this is expected behavior and doesn't provide the concrete solutions (persist_result still works, use create_*_artifact methods) given in the reference.

PrefectHQ__prefect.title: prefect support MS SQL Server as Database
PrefectHQ__prefect.reason: Candidate correctly states MSSQL is unsupported and recommends Azure PostgreSQL, fully matching the reference.

PrefectHQ__prefect.title: Subflow Task Runner Inheritance
PrefectHQ__prefect.reason: Candidate correctly states task runners aren't inherited and recommends with_options matching the reference, but its option 3 claim that a child without task_runner uses the parent's context is incorrect.

PrefectHQ__prefect.title: Remote storage for workers
PrefectHQ__prefect.reason: The candidate correctly notes remote storage support is a roadmap question but fails to confirm it's coming or give the ~5-10 week estimate, instead deflecting to external channels.

PrefectHQ__prefect.title: Problem authentication with DockerRegistry
PrefectHQ__prefect.reason: The real issue was a typo (registry vs image_registry attribute), which the candidate completely missed, even reinforcing the wrong attribute name.

PrefectHQ__prefect.title: Orion picks up sqlalchemy.py and crashes - any idea why?
PrefectHQ__prefect.reason: Candidate correctly explains the filename-shadowing circular import and matches the reference's root cause with helpful detail.

PrefectHQ__prefect.title: Docker Storage equivalent for Orioin
PrefectHQ__prefect.reason: The reference is a roadmap/awareness answer; the candidate misidentifies Orion and gives generic deflection but does correctly suggest checking release notes.

PrefectHQ__prefect.title: Logging under Windows 10 not working?
PrefectHQ__prefect.reason: Candidate correctly identifies the missing `import prefect` as the cause, matching the reference answer and adding accurate explanation.

litestar-org__litestar.title: Basic Auth
litestar-org__litestar.reason: The candidate gives only generic pseudocode and asks for framework details, failing to provide the Litestar-specific AbstractAuthenticationMiddleware and OpenAPI SecurityScheme solution.

litestar-org__litestar.title: Implementing a Payload Validation after the Response Body is generated with a token
litestar-org__litestar.reason: The candidate recommends middleware, which the reference explicitly says won't work since the body is already serialized, missing the custom response class solution.

litestar-org__litestar.title: redirect to different route_handler from a route_handler
litestar-org__litestar.reason: Candidate misses the key answer (route_reverse to get path for redirect) and instead suggests extracting shared logic, even doubting Litestar support.

litestar-org__litestar.title: How do I generate typescript types for a recursive data type?
litestar-org__litestar.reason: The candidate misses the Litestar-specific solution (using a DTO with ClassicPagination) and instead suggests generic, unrelated workarounds.

litestar-org__litestar.title: Where to find more Litestar examples for the intermediate developer?
litestar-org__litestar.reason: The reference answer indicates no such intermediate example exists yet (it's a roadmap/discussion item), while the candidate suggests search strategies that don't acknowledge this reality, though it correctly points to community channels.

litestar-org__litestar.title: Accessing request data in guards
litestar-org__litestar.reason: Candidate misses the key answer—connection.json() works in guards—and wrongly claims body can't be accessed in guards, though its handler/dependency alternatives partially overlap with the reference.

litestar-org__litestar.title: Excluding None (optional fields) from json response bodies
litestar-org__litestar.reason: Reference confirms manual dict conversion is the only way; candidate fabricates non-existent config options that contradict the correct answer.

litestar-org__litestar.title: Bug: query params doesn't work?
litestar-org__litestar.reason: Candidate correctly identifies the zsh shell glob issue and provides the same quoting/escaping fix as the reference.

litestar-org__litestar.title: Understanding the role of advanced-alchemy
litestar-org__litestar.reason: The candidate correctly explains advanced-alchemy's features but misses key points (it's the same code as old contrib bundled standalone, non-intrusive design, multi-framework reuse) and frames the inconsistency as a real problem the reference rebuts.

litestar-org__litestar.title: Raising HTTPException vs returning Response
litestar-org__litestar.reason: Candidate correctly explains the control-flow distinction and confirms the deliberate serialization difference with valid workarounds, though it frames it as an 'inconsistency' rather than an intentional low-level design choice.

litestar-org__litestar.title: Can't see exception tracebacks in pytest with TestClient
litestar-org__litestar.reason: The correct fix is debug=True; in Litestar the exception is handled internally so raise_server_exceptions=True doesn't surface the traceback, making the candidate's primary solution wrong.

litestar-org__litestar.title: How to define "endpoint" without using "decorator syntax" ?
litestar-org__litestar.reason: The candidate answers about Starlette instead of Litestar, completely missing that the question is about Litestar's decorator-based route handlers.

litestar-org__litestar.title: Changing operation names for handlers in the schema
litestar-org__litestar.reason: Candidate correctly identifies operation_id as the solution matching the reference, though it adds unnecessary framework-hedging and questions.

litestar-org__litestar.title: MongoDB / Beanie Integration
litestar-org__litestar.reason: Reference just points to discussion #1072; candidate gives generic, partly inaccurate (AsyncIOMotorClient) advice not matching the reference.

marimo-team__marimo.title: 🐛 Bug ❓: Variable name collision for SQL cells using same table name in different schemas
marimo-team__marimo.reason: The reference says it's a confirmed bug fixed in the next release, but the candidate treats it as an existing limitation and proposes workarounds without identifying it as a fixed bug.

marimo-team__marimo.title: Accordion elements visible by default
marimo-team__marimo.reason: The candidate fabricates a non-existent `open` parameter, whereas the reference confirms no such feature exists.

marimo-team__marimo.title: Sharing notebooks on the same machine? I.e. workaround for `Access denied: File is outside the allowed directory.`
marimo-team__marimo.reason: The reference says it's a bug fixed in an upcoming PR, while the candidate invents unverified flags, config, and workarounds that don't match the actual fix.

marimo-team__marimo.title: custom css in FastAPI deployment?
marimo-team__marimo.reason: The reference points to the pyproject.toml configuration method, which the candidate never mentions, instead offering incorrect guesses.

marimo-team__marimo.title: nvidia-smi logs clogging output
marimo-team__marimo.reason: The candidate misses the actual solution (setting log level via `marimo -l ERROR`) and invents non-existent env vars and config options, with only the grep fallback being a partially valid workaround.

marimo-team__marimo.title: why I cannot use underscore as variable name in marimo
marimo-team__marimo.reason: The candidate is factually wrong—underscore variables are allowed but cell-local, not reserved/forbidden as it claims.

marimo-team__marimo.title: How to keep the script running after closing the browser?
marimo-team__marimo.reason: The candidate correctly identifies the same APScheduler/BackgroundScheduler solution plus broader workarounds, but this is more of an architecture/workaround question than a docs-derivable one.

marimo-team__marimo.title: How to enable sqlite introspection in datasource pane
marimo-team__marimo.reason: The reference answer reveals this was a bug fixed in version 0.11.18, while the candidate gives generic troubleshooting without identifying the version-based fix.

marimo-team__marimo.title: Looking for a way to statically host several notebooks, without having an 18M directory per notebook.
marimo-team__marimo.reason: The candidate misses the actual solution (the marimo gh-pages template with shared assets folder) and instead suggests caching and manual workarounds.

marimo-team__marimo.title: Exporting multiple notebooks to html-wasm
marimo-team__marimo.reason: Candidate correctly confirms shared assets and the proposed file structure work, matching the reference, though it adds some unverified caveats.

marimo-team__marimo.title: Can I use marimo as Streamlit to write full-stack applications
marimo-team__marimo.reason: Candidate correctly affirms marimo can be used like Streamlit but adds unsupported claims about limitations and misses marimo's actual full-stack/app deployment capabilities and the doc comparison link.

marimo-team__marimo.title: button clear multiselect
marimo-team__marimo.reason: The candidate gives generic React/JS solutions, completely missing marimo's approach of constructing the multiselect and button in the same cell.

marimo-team__marimo.title: can't show plots using matplotlib
marimo-team__marimo.reason: The reference says it was a bug fixed in a March update, while the candidate assumes Jupyter (not marimo) and gives irrelevant generic workarounds.

marimo-team__marimo.title: How do you style the main 'body' or 'root' id?
marimo-team__marimo.reason: The candidate gives generic CSS/React advice but misses marimo's actual mechanism (mo.Html with a style block) and the caveat about non-guaranteed classnames.

duckdb__duckdb.title: Do/should macros work in the WHERE clause
duckdb__duckdb.reason: The candidate misdiagnoses this as a macro limitation and gives wrong advice, missing the actual name-resolution/column-shadowing issue and the qualify-the-argument fix.

duckdb__duckdb.title: How to disable filter pushdown into join nodes?
duckdb__duckdb.reason: Candidate gives wrong/irrelevant settings for other databases and never mentions DuckDB's actual workarounds (PRAGMA disable_optimizer, subquery blocking).

duckdb__duckdb.title: querying tables in Microsoft .mdb files using python duckdb
duckdb__duckdb.reason: Candidate fabricates a non-existent DuckDB ODBC extension/read_odbc, contradicting the reference that DuckDB cannot connect to ODBC; only the pandas-bridge option is correct.

duckdb__duckdb.title: Converting `TIMESTAMPTZ`s to `DATE`s in a specified time zone
duckdb__duckdb.reason: The AT TIME ZONE approach is correct and equivalent to timezone(), but the answer wrongly hedges that this is PostgreSQL syntax that may differ in DuckDB.

duckdb__duckdb.title: Supporting nested aggregates
duckdb__duckdb.reason: Candidate misses the key insight (the original works with explicit group-by/struct names), uses incorrect unnest-struct destructuring `u(state,amt)` and a nonexistent `list_agg` function, and mainly proposes the cumbersome CTE workaround the user already rejected.

duckdb__duckdb.title: Problem Querying JSON Array of Objects with UNNEST
duckdb__duckdb.reason: The candidate correctly identifies the subquery-scoping fix (wrapping UNNEST in a FROM subquery) and json_extract_string is a valid approach, but it relies on the nonexistent `json_parse` function instead of `::JSON[]`/`from_json`, so the SQL would error as written.

duckdb__duckdb.title: Arrow Extension Type conversion and nulls
duckdb__duckdb.reason: Reference is just a bug fix PR; candidate correctly identifies it as a DuckDB bug in extension-type null handling but can't cite the fix.

duckdb__duckdb.title: Support for Length-Specified VARCHAR in read_csv_auto Column Definitions with rejects_errors in DuckDB
duckdb__duckdb.reason: Candidate correctly states VARCHAR(n) length is accepted but not enforced (matching the reference) and offers valid post-load workarounds.

duckdb__duckdb.title: Iceberg Glue supports only S3Tables ?
duckdb__duckdb.reason: Reference just redirects to the duckdb-iceberg repo; candidate instead gives speculative claims not aligned with that pointer.

duckdb__duckdb.title: Querying duckdb through GUI tool?
duckdb__duckdb.reason: Candidate correctly recommends DBeaver matching the reference and adds accurate additional GUI options.

duckdb__duckdb.title: Setting working directory when connecting from java jdbc
duckdb__duckdb.reason: Candidate gives generic Java path workarounds and misses DuckDB's relative-path/working-directory behavior the reference documents.

duckdb__duckdb.title: DuckDB C API: Arrow functions
duckdb__duckdb.reason: The reference clarifies deprecation does not yet have an alternative, while the candidate fabricates a migration path and nonexistent functions, contradicting the correct answer.

duckdb__duckdb.title: attach sqlite db, what engine does the work?
duckdb__duckdb.reason: Candidate correctly states DuckDB's engine does the work and scans the SQLite file, matching the reference with accurate extra detail.

duckdb__duckdb.title: Problem with "inconsistent sorting"
duckdb__duckdb.reason: Candidate correctly diagnoses non-stable/non-deterministic sorting with tied keys and offers valid deterministic-ordering fixes, but misses the reference's specific workaround (SET threads=1).
